content:
## Reference Wiki and Evaluation Framework

A reference wiki should be written manually as ground truth, then different generation strategies evaluated against it. The evaluation needs multiple checks: is the right content included, are the invariants present, are the pages structured sensibly, are they in the right categories. The reference wiki already exists in git history from the initial manual creation. This approach applies the characterisation test pattern to wiki generation itself. [[source:811302f6-0be7-435c-b844-910cc9a21b67/50]]

provenance.raw_agent: This is essentially applying the characterisation test approach to the wiki generation itself. The reference wiki is the ground truth, different strategies are evaluated against it.
provenance.raw_user: I'm not sure what the best way of generating the wiki is. It might be that we write one version of it as a reference, using all the context from this session, then we try different strategies for generating it. We would need some mechanism for checking that everything is included.
